name: My back hurts from lifting boxes at my Boise Idaho job but symptoms started days later, is this still a work injury claim?

mainEntity.name: My back hurts from lifting boxes at my Boise Idaho job but symptoms started days later, is this still a work injury claim?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, delayed back pain from work lifting qualifies in Idaho if reported promptly and medically linked.[2]
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, delayed symptoms like back strains from work lifting qualify for Idaho workers' comp if linked to the job.[2] Injuries can worsen over time, so insurers may question causation without prompt medical records.[2] Key factors: report immediately (within 60 days max); tell doctor it's work-related; follow treatment to prove ongoing impact.[2] Get evaluated now, document job duties, and get legal help to connect delayed symptoms to work for full benefits.[3]

description: Yes, delayed back pain from work lifting qualifies in Idaho if reported promptly and medically linked.[2]
